title: DVWA Navigation Guide
when_to_use: When exploring web vulnerabilities in a safe lab environment

steps.title: Start & Login
steps.instruction: Start DVWA and log in with default credentials (admin/password)
steps.tip: If credentials don't work, check DVWA documentation for your version

steps.title: Set Security Level
steps.instruction: Set security level to LOW via DVWA Security tab
steps.tip: Start at LOW to understand the vulnerability. Increase to MEDIUM and HIGH later to learn defences

steps.title: Navigate Modules
steps.instruction: Navigate to each module: SQLi, XSS Reflected, Command Injection
steps.tip: Read the Help section for each module — it explains what the vulnerability is and how it works

steps.title: Normal Usage First
steps.instruction: For each module, first use it normally to understand intended function
steps.tip: Understanding normal behaviour is essential — you can't spot anomalies without a baseline

steps.title: Test Unexpected Input
steps.instruction: Then enter unexpected input: single quotes, angle brackets, semicolons
steps.tip: Start simple: ' (single quote) for SQLi, test for XSS, ;ls for command injection

steps.title: Screenshot Responses
steps.instruction: Observe and screenshot the response for each input type
steps.tip: Compare normal and abnormal responses side by side. The difference reveals the vulnerability

steps.title: Isolation Warning
steps.instruction: Never run DVWA on a network-connected machine without isolation
steps.tip: DVWA is intentionally vulnerable. On a network, it's a real security risk. Always use host-only networking
